questions.questionText: What is the purpose of crimping in WISP installations?
options: To attach connectors to cables for secure signal transmission

questions.questionText: Which tool is commonly used to crimp RJ45 connectors onto Ethernet cables?
What cable type is typically used in a WISP setup to connect CPE devices?
Why is it important to test a crimped cable before installation?
options: To check the brand of the cable
To ensure all wires are properly connected and transmitting signal

questions.questionText: What does 'T568B' refer to in networking?
options: A wiring standard for Ethernet cables

questions.questionText: Improper crimping can lead to which of the following issues?
Which type of cable is best suited for outdoor WISP installations?
options: Shielded Ethernet cable with UV protection

questions.questionText: What is a common tool used to remove insulation from wires before crimping?
Why is shielding important in Ethernet cables for WISP use?
options: It adds color to the cable
It protects the wires from water damage

questions.questionText: What does a cable tester do in the context of WISP installations?
options: Connects a client to the internet
Secures the cable to the wall
Verifies the integrity and pinout of a crimped cable
